titlestmt.titleproper: Gale Sondergaard and Herbert Biberman papers LSC.2446
titlestmt.author: Finding aid prepared by Sage Scanlon-Perez in the Center for Primary Research Training (CFPRT), under the supervision of Emily Wittenberg, 2026.

address.addressline: Room A1713, Charles E. Young Research Library

publicationstmt.p: Finding aid description and metadata in this record are licensed under a Creative Commons Public Domain Designation (CC0 1.0 Universal). See LSC's Metadata Rights Statement for more information.

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2026-07-17 14:36:22 -0700.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: English, Latin script.

did.unittitle: Gale Sondergaard and Herbert Biberman papers
did.abstract:
Gale Sondergaard (1899-1985), was an actor and screenwriter. She began her acting career in the theater and made her film debut in 1936, winning the first-ever Academy Award for Best Supporting Actress. Her husband was the screenwriter and director Herbert Biberman (1900-1971). Both were involved in left-leaning causes throughout the 1930s and 1940s and subsequently called before the House Un-American Activities Committee (HUAC) for alleged Communist Party sympathies. In 1947 Biberman was indicted, as one of the Hollywood 10, for contempt and sentenced to 6 months in prison. Sondergaard testified in 1951 and was blacklisted for invoking her 5th Amendment rights. Their persecution is indicative of how HUAC hunted for alleged communist conspiracies and conspirators in all corners of society, but with particular vigor in Hollywood's motion picture industry.
This collection is comprised of Sondergaard's correspondence, statements, personal and professional writings, press coverage and photographs. The collection also documents Biberman's career through professional writings, planners, memoir, and photographs.
Some portions of the collection were not explicitly created by Sondergaard or Biberman, nor explicitly speak to their careers, but rather provide context to the red scare that tore up Hollywood throughout the 1930s/40s. These records include an extensive assortment of press clippings and coverage documenting the trial of the Hollywood 10, blacklisting, and HUAC, along with HUAC related pamphlets and leaflets.
did.physloc: Stored off-site. All requests to access special collections material must be made in advance using the request button located on this page.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Open for research. All requests to access special collections materials must be made in advance using the request button located on this page.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Property rights to the physical objects belong to UCLA Library Special Collections. All other rights, including copyright, are retained by the creators and their heirs. It is the responsibility of the researcher to determine who holds the copyright and pursue the copyright owner or his or her heir for permission to publish where The UC Regents do not hold the copyright.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased at auction from Swann Auction Galleries, 2018.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: [Identification of item], Gale Sondergaard and Herbert Biberman papers (Collection 2446). UCLA Library Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, University of California, Los Angeles.

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p: Materials consigned to Swann Galleries by a cousin of Herbert Biberman who worked for Gale Sondergaard as her personal assistant from 1974 until her death in 1985. The items in the collection were acquired directly from the Sondergaard sisters by gift or purchase.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Processed by Sage Scanlon-Perez, in the Center for Primary Research and Training (CFPRT), under the supervision of Emily Wittenberg, 2026.

Original order was preserved where discernible and original folder titles were retained where present and where they accurately reflected the contents. In some cases, titles were supplied, revised, or standardized for clarity, consistency, and description.

Collections are processed to a variety of levels depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived user interest and research value, availability of staff and resources, and competing priorities. Library Special Collections provides a standard level of preservation and access for all collections and, when time and resources permit, conducts more intensive processing. These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards and best practices

We are committed to providing ethical, inclusive, and anti-racist description of the materials we steward, and to remediating existing description of our materials that contains language that may be offensive or cause harm. We invite you to submit feedback about how our collections are described, and how they could be described more accurately, by filling out the form located on our website: Report Problematic Content and Description in UCLA's library collections and archives.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Gale Sondergaard (1899-1985), was an actor and screenwriter. She began her acting career in the theater and made her film debut in 1936, winning the first-ever Academy Award for Best Supporting Actress. Her husband was the screenwriter and director Herbert Biberman (1900-1971). Both were involved in left-leaning causes in the 1930s and 1940s. Both were called before the House Un-American Activities Committee (HUAC) for alleged Communist Party sympathies, Biberman in 1947 and Sondergaard in 1951. Biberman was one of ten screenwriters who refused to testify, and like other members of the Hollywood Ten was blacklisted and sentenced to six months in prison in 1950. Sondergaard was blacklisted after invoking the Fifth Amendment. She was not permitted to work in Hollywood again until 1969. Their persecution was just one part of a larger agenda by the United States government to root out left-leaning and communist party members during the red scare. By documenting the rise and fall of Sondergaard and Biberman's careers this collection highlights how HUAC led assaults against anyone suspected of being insubordinate.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: This collection was originally housed with a clear distinction between Gale Sondergaard's papers and Herbert Biberman's. The Sondergaard papers include correspondence, statements, personal and professional writings, press coverage and photographs. Biberman papers include personal and professional writings, scripts, memoirs, planners, notes, photographs. Originally included alongside Biberman papers was a small collection of books that have since been removed and catalogued individually, now available for use via UCLA Library database.

Some portions of the collection were not explicitly created by Sondergaard or Biberman, nor explicitly documenting their careers, but rather provide context to the red scare that tore through Hollywood in the 1930s/40s. These records were clearly labeled as relating to the House Un-American Activities Committee (HUAC), documenting the trial of the Hollywood 10, blacklisting, and the red scare through press clippings and coverage, pamphlets, and leaflets.
A smaller portion of the collection documents some of the career of Gale Sondergaard's sister, Hester Sondergaard through photographs, playbills, press, and some correspondence.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p:
This collection has been arranged in the following series:
Series 1: Gale Sondergaard Records (1939-1989)
Series 2: General Records (1932-1985)
Series 3: Herbert Biberman Records (1921-1971)
Subseries 3.1: Personal Papers(1921-1971)
Subseries 3.2: Professional Papers (1944-1968)
Series 4: House Un-American Activities Committee Records (1942-1989)
Series 5: Photographs (1927-1978)
Subseries 5.1: Gale Sondergaard Photographs (1936-1978)
Subseries 5.2: General Photographs (1927-1949)
Subseries 5.3: Herbert Biberman Photographs (1945)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Documents the personal and professional sides of Gale Sondergaard's life through correspondence, personal notes, press clippings, and professional writing. Includes records documenting her navigation of the HUAC subpoena and statement, the subsequent impact on her career, and her successes later in life.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Magazines maintained in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mostly contains typed letters from Gale Sondergaard addressed to "all my dears." Also includes some handwritten notes and letters.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a folder maintained in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Condolences shared with Gale Sondergaard on the passing of her husband, Herbert Biberman.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a file maintained in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typed copy of Sondergaard's unpublished autobiography.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a copy the The Village Voice with an article titled 'Blacklisted: The Odyssey and Comeback of Gale Sondergaard.'

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a file maintained in original order.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a file maintained in original order.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a file maintained in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains speech at the Embassy Auditorium, handwritten notes, and a diary.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a copy of Gale Sondergaard's letter to the board of directors of the Screen Actors Guild concerning her subpoena and the board's passivity to HUAC's investigations. Also includes a copy of the board of directors' response.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two programs, "an arresting program of readings from the modern era of the theatre in which woman emerges as a full-flavored human being."

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a disassembled binder maintained in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Documents some of Hester Sondergaard's career as a radio voice actress and performer. Provides context on other artists and projects of the time through press clippings, pamphlets, and general correspondence.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes the obituary of Lester Cole, one of the Hollywood Ten. Mostly contains press clippings on theatre workshops and techniques.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a file maintained in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Press coverage of Hester Sondergaard's radio and theater career.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a file maintained in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes professional correspondence with various managers and producers as well as personal correspondence. Also contains Hester Sondergaard's work history and resume.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a file maintained in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes pamphlets from the American Federation of Television and Radio Artists, The Press-Freedom's Test 50th Anniversary ACLU 1973 Bill of Rights Journal.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a folder maintained in original order.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a folder maintained in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains programs for different Russian War Relief events.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes some productions featuring Gale Sondergaard.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes some programs with work by Gale Sondergaard and Herbert Biberman.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a folder maintained in original order.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a file maintained in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes press coverage of Bitter Stream, Marching Song, and Black Pit. Coverage includes information about Hester Sondergaard's role as Mary Lakavitch in Black Pit.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a folder maintained in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Documents the professional and personal writings of Biberman, providing insight into his career and relationship with Gale Sondergaard.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials were originally housed thematically, the sub-series reflect that original order. Folder names were maintained when discernible and appropriate, arranged here alphabetically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Documents the professional writings of Herbert Biberman as a scriptwriter.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Maintained by original binding.

scopecontent.p: Documents the personal writings and musings of Biberman through personal planners, notes, correspondence, and a memoir.

did.unittitle: The California Quarterly (Salt of the Earth)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Volume 2, Number 4 of the pamphlet that includes the screen play Salt of the Earth. One edition contains handwritten edits and notes. The other contains press related to the play.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Maintained in original order by their original binding. One edition contains additional loose notes and press clippings that are maintained in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A memoir by Herbert Biberman written for Gale Sondergaard, "for our twenty-five years."

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Maintained by original binding in original order.

scopecontent.p: Part 2: America.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Maintained in original order by its original binding.

scopecontent.p: Part 3: Un-America!

scopecontent.p: Part 4: The World.

arrangement.head: Arrangement

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes the birth certificate and obituary of Herbert Biberman.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes planners from the third and fourth trimesters of 1962. Also includes an address book.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Planners maintained in original order by their original binding. The address book contains loose notes and books that are maintained in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains handwritten notes detailing meetings, appointments, social commitments, and personal musings.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Maintained in original order by their original binding.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains handwritten notes detailing meetings, appointments, social commitments, and personal musings.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Maintained in original order by their original binding.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Four pocket planners covering the entire year.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Maintained in original order by their original binding.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains handwritten notes detailing appointments, meetings, and personal musings.

arrangement.head: Arrangement

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a bookmark highlighting the page with a write up on "Herb."

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Maintained in original order by its original binding.

did.unittitle: Series 4: House Un-American Activities Committee Records

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters and notices from the National Council of the Arts, Sciences and Professions, American Federation of Radio Artists, and Counterattack.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically.

did.unittitle: Hester Sondergaard's Notebook of Blacklist Victims

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a leaflet titled 'To a Friend of the Hollywood Ten' collectively penned by the wives of the Hollywood Ten. Also contains several pamphlets about the blacklist including, 'What Does the Blacklist Mean?' and 'How to Fight the Blacklist.'

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a file maintained in original order with their original label.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a pamphlet titled 'Ring Lardner, Jr. Tells Reasons FOr Stand of Hollywood Ten.' Also includes other pamphlets about the blacklist.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a file maintained in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains pamphlets created by members of the Hollywood 10 and their wives like 'The Time of the Toad a Study of Inquisition in America by One of the Hollywood Ten' by Dalton Trumbo. Also 'On the Eve of Prison Two Addresses by Gale Sondergaard and Albert Maltz.'

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a file maintained in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes press coverage of the Hollywood Ten and Gale Sondergaard with articles such as 'The Hollywood Ten Ask Americans to Speak Out' and 'Lawson's Guilty Verdict Augurs Ill for the Other 9; Trumbo Next.' An article featuring an interview with Gale Sondergaard is titled 'I Am An American.'

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a file maintained in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes coverage about the Hollywood Ten and the red scare in Hollywood with titles like 'Film Notables Urge End to Un-Americans at Rally Here' and 'The Arithmetic of Terrorism Example: The Hollywood Ten Minus Two Leaves Eight, Where does that leave YOU?' Includes a 'Consolidated List of Organizations and Groups Designated Totalitarian, Fascist, Communist or Subversive by the Attorney General of the United States.' Also contains coverage about the blacklist and Gale Sondergaard.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a file maintained in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes coverage of the blacklist and the repercussions on individuals and their families with articles like 'Children of the Blacklist Growing up During Hollywood's Most Shameful Era.'

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a folder maintained in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes coverage of the red scare in Hollywood. Titles of articles include '5 Convicted Reds Get 5-Year Terms' and 'Case Study Reveals Un-American Tactics of Un-American Committee.'

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a folder maintained in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes coverage of the HUAC trials, the red scare, and Gale Sondergaard.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a folder maintained in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains coverage of red scare and blacklist including titles like 'Red in '46, Sterling Hayden Says; He Names Others' and 'Actor Sterling Hayden Admits He Was Red' and 'Ohio Gets New State Anti-Subversive Law.'

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a file maintained in original order.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a file maintained in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes statements by Laurent Casanova and Vincent Hallinan.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a file maintained in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Documents the prolific career of Gale Sondergaard through photographs, portraits and stills of her multitude or roles in the motion picture industry. A smaller portion of the series documents some of Herbert Biberman's and Hester Sondegaard's projects. Additionally documents the personal lives of Gale Sondergaard and Biberman through photographs of them as a couple, and their family unit.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: A majority of the photographs were clearly labeled and organized around Gale Sondergaard's projects. Arrangement of the sub-series reflects that organization, with original labels maintained when discernible. This series is broken into three sub-series reflecting original organization of the photographs depicting Gale Sondergaard, general, and Herbert Biberman.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Documents the extensive career of Gale Sondergaard through her performances in various motion picture and theater productions.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Photographs were housed separately from other materials and thoroughly labeled based on motion picture film/theater projects. This is reflected in the arrangement of this series that maintains those original labels, and dates when available, arranged here alphabetically.

did.unittitle: Anna and the King of Siam (Photographs/Stills)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Depicts Gale Sondergaard in her Academy Award nominated role for best supporting actress as Lady Thiang. Some stills also depict Irene Dunne.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a box maintained under their original label in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains stills depicting Gale Sondergaard in her role as Faith and Claude Rains as Don Luis in Anthony Adverse.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a box maintained under their original label in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs of Gale Sondergaard in character alongside Johnny Russell, Eddie Collins and Shirley Temple.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a box maintained under their original label in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photographs and stills showcasing Gale Sondergaard in her role as the spirit-haunted housekeeper in Paramount's picturization of the famous mystery comedy The Cat and the Canary, co-starring Bob Hope and Paulette Goddard.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a box maintained under their original label in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs of Gale Sondergaard as Luise, the doctor's housekeeper in The Climax.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mostly portraits solely featuring Sondergaard. Includes a candid of Sondergaard and Biberman at a premiere.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains candids, portraits, and some photographs from unidentified events and projects.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a file maintained in original order in their original folder.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains portraits of a young Gale Sondergaard along with an unidentified young man.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a box maintained under original label in original order.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a box maintained under their original label in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains portraits of Gale Sondergaard throughout her life, including some from her various roles in unidentified projects.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a box maintained under their original label in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes stills of Gale Sondergaard in her role of Emily the housemaid, alongside Lou Costello as Horatio Prim, and Bud Abbott as Dr. Ralph Greenway.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a box maintained under their original label in original order.

arrangement.head: Arrangement

arrangement.p: Contents of a box maintained under their original label in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes stills of former blacklisted actress Gale Sondergaard taking the Fifth Amendment before the 1951 House Committee on Un-American Activities in Hollywood on Trial.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a box maintained under their original label in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes stills of Gale Sondergaard in her role of Irene alongside Jon Hall as Griffin, and Lester Matthews as Jasper.

arrangement.p: Contents of a box maintained under their original label in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains stills showcasing Gale Sondergaard in her role as the madam.

did.unittitle: The Life of Emile Zola Premier (Photographs)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes multiple photographs of Gale Sondergaard escorted by her husband Herbert Biberman at the Hollywood premiere of Warner Bros'. The Life of Emile Zola.

arrangement.p: Contents of a box maintained under their original label in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes stills of Freddie Batholomew and Gale Sondergaard in a dramatic moment in Metro-Goldwyn-Mayer's Lord Jeff.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes stills of Gale Sondergaard and Bob Hope in character.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a box maintained under their original label in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a photograph of Gale Sondergaard resting between scenes on the Never Say Die set at Paramount, where she was working alongside Bob Hope, Martha Raye, Andy Devine and others. In this comedy Sondergaard plays the first burlesque role of her film career.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a box maintained under their original label in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photographs of Gale Sondergaard in her role as Atossa, the sorceress.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a box maintained under their original label in original order.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a box maintained under their original label in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photographs and stills of Gale Sondergaard in her role as Collette in Univerisal's Paris Calling.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a box maintained under their original label in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains stills of Gale Sondergaard in her role as Senora De Sola.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a box maintained under their original label in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes stills of Gale Sondergaard as Catherine Vail, Bing Crosby as Seat Sweeney and Bob Hope as Hot Lips.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a box maintained under their original label in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes stills of Gale Sondergaard in her role of Marguerite Beaulac on ABC's daytime dramatic series, Ryan's Hope.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a box maintained under their original label in original order.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a box maintained under their original label in original order.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a box maintained under their original label in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes stills of Gale Sondergaard in her role as Zenobia Dollard alongside Brenda Joyce as Jean Kinglsey and Rondo Hatton as Mario.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a box maintained under their original label in original order.

did.unittitle: The Strange Death of Adolf Hitler (Photographs/Stills)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs and stills of Gale Sondergaard in her role as the wife of Hitler's double, and George Dolenz in his portrayal of his best friend. Universal Studios production.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a box maintained under their original label in original order.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a box maintained under their original label in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes individual stills depicting Gale Sondergaard in her role in The Black Cat, Seventh Heaven, Visions, Follow the Boys, and various other unidentified projects.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Documents the personal side of Gale Sondergaard and Herbert Biberman through family photographs, and photographs of the couple. Includes photographs showcasing portions of Hester Sondergaard's career in television.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mostly contains photographs of Gale Sondergaard with her and Biberman's adopted children Joan Kirstine and Daniel Hans.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Candids and portraits of the couple.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains candids and stills of Hester Sondergaard from unidentified events and projects.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs from unidentified events and projects. Contains some portraits of unidentified people addressed to Gale Sondergaard.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs of Gale Sondergaard and Hester Sondergaard in various unidentified theater and motion picture productions.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Documents the professional projects and successes of Biberman in the film industry through photographs and stills.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Associate producer Herbert Biberman.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photographs depicting Herbert Biberman on set and behind the scenes during the production of The Master Race.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a box maintained in original order under their original label.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a photograph of Biberman giving Peggy Conklin directions for a scene.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a box maintained under their original label in original order.
